description: Retune the default bulk-prefix cutoff from 315 to 314. On the current scaffold, shaving off that last specialized iteration is a free win: 4,163,102 -> 4,162,746 Toffoli with qubits unchanged at 2716, and the built-in alternate-seed gate plus checks both pass.

asi.hypothesis: The specialized bulk-prefix tail may have drifted slightly suboptimal after later scaffold changes, so the current default k=315 could be one iteration too long.
asi.learning: That tail really is over-tuned by exactly one step here: k=314 is repeatedly stable and 356 Toffoli cheaper than k=315, while nearby values are non-monotone (k=304 phase-fails, k=320 regresses). The optimum needs local retuning after structural changes; old prefix values do not transfer cleanly.
asi.validation: Two identical env-gated probe runs at k=314 matched exactly before flipping the default.
asi.next_action_hint: Probe the immediately adjacent region only if worthwhile (313/314/315/316). The surface is jagged, so larger jumps waste runs.

description: Re-enable off-build structural testing by fixing the stale `fermat_inv` test compile break and add `src/point_add/kim_costs.md` with measured Kim inversion costs. Top-level benchmark stays unchanged at 4,162,414 / 2716q, but we can now invalidate the direct-Kim path quickly: the existing Bennett-clean Kim inversion primitive measures 2,530,240 Toffoli at 4102 peak qubits, so a simple live drop-in is dead for the 1200q target.

asi.hypothesis: Before deeper structural work, make the dormant Kim test path runnable again so we can measure and invalidate major architecture options directly instead of reasoning from stale notes.
asi.learning: Hard measured result now preserved in-tree: Kim forward inversion at n=256 is 1,252,352 Toffoli / 3590q, and the full Bennett-clean `kim_inv(x,out)` primitive is 2,530,240 Toffoli / 4102q. That makes the current wide-state Kim route a qubit non-starter for the ~1200q target even though its Toffoli is not absurd.
asi.structural_result: Direct wide-state Kim drop-in is dead; if Kim ideas survive at all, they must be folded into a much more compact shared-state/coset/windowed architecture.
asi.benchmark_effect: none on live build; benchmark/checks still pass at current best 4,162,414 / 2716q.
asi.next_action_hint: Pivot from direct Kim wiring toward lower-state architectural ingredients: compact register-sharing, QROM/windowing infrastructure, or finishing missing constant-workspace adder pieces such as 3-clean composition.

description: Add exact secp256k1 coset/padded prototype infrastructure (`src/point_add/coset_proto.rs` + `coset_proto.md`) and use it to falsify the easiest coset landing zone. For short 3-8 add chains, padded non-mod adds plus one Bennett-clean canonicalization are WORSE than direct modular adds: about +1k Toffoli fixed overhead and +500q (qb adds) to +770q (qq adds). Live benchmark remains unchanged at 4,162,414 / 2716q.

asi.hypothesis: A minimal exact coset-style prototype might already pay off on short affine correction chains if we replace several modular adds with padded non-mod adds and one final canonicalization.
asi.learning: It does not. On real emitted-gate counts, our current modular add primitives are already cheap enough that a one-time exact canonicalization dominates. Measured deltas: qb-chain reps=3/8 cost +1030/+1072 CCX and +514/+516q; qq-chain reps=3/8 cost +1030/+1072 CCX and +770/+772q. So short chains are the wrong first landing spot for coset arithmetic.
asi.structural_result: Coset/padded arithmetic should only be pursued on LONG arithmetic regions where cleanup is amortized: inversion internals, QROM-windowed batches, or a wider non-canonical scaffold. The naive short-chain insertion point is dead.
asi.next_action_hint: If continuing the coset line, target a long arithmetic region or pair it with QROM/windowing. Otherwise pivot back to non-coset structural wins.

description: Extend the coset/padded prototype sweep to locate the actual amortization crossover. The prototype still loses on short chains, but now we know where it starts to help: around 12 repeated adds it saves Toffoli (~952 CCX at 12 reps, ~1664 CCX at 16 reps) while still costing roughly +516q for qb chains and +772q for qq chains. Benchmark unchanged at 4,162,414 / 2716q.

asi.hypothesis: If exact coset cleanup is a mostly fixed overhead, the padded prototype should eventually cross over and beat repeated direct modular adds on sufficiently long chains.
asi.learning: Confirmed. For both qb and qq chains the crossover is around 12 repeated adds: reps=12 gives -952 CCX, reps=16 gives -1664 CCX. But the qubit tax remains severe: about +516q for qb chains and +772q for qq chains. So coset arithmetic is only plausible when (a) a long add/sub region exists and (b) the padded accumulator can REUSE already-live wide workspace instead of stacking on top of the current live set.
asi.structural_result: This turns the coset line from a blanket dead-end into a constrained target: not short affine fixups, but long arithmetic regions with reusable wide state.
asi.next_action_hint: Look for a region with 12+ modular adds/subs and an existing wide temporary we could reinterpret as the padded accumulator — most likely inside multiplication reduction or a future windowed/QROM batch.

description: Narrow with_gt comparator in bulk-prefix STEP2 (both forward and backward) to the late-iter Kaliski bound max(2n-k, n). u and v_w high bits are 0 by invariant, so a truncated prefix comparison is classically equivalent and preserves phase. 4,143,262 → 4,136,878 (-6,384). Checks pass.

asi.hypothesis: kal_bulk_step2 and bk_bulk_step2 use with_gt(u, v_w, ...) at full n width. For iter_idx >= n, the Kaliski invariant bitlen(u)+bitlen(v_w) ≤ 2n-iter_idx forces u[i]=v_w[i]=0 for i >= 2n-iter_idx, so a truncated comparator gives the same result.
asi.next_action_hint: Try similar truncation on the full-n OR chain in kal_step0_eqzero / bk_step0_eqzero (non-bulk iters) if it isn't already truncated. Also check if cmp_lt_into_fast inside with_gt has an internal carry-chain allocation that could shrink with smaller inputs.
